mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving while managing memory efficiently. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of configuration for advanced features.

steps.title: Add Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

```

> This command sets up the repository for the latest vLLM release.

steps.title: Install vLLM CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for vLLM to function correctly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> CRDs define the custom resources used by vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace with the defined version.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> Ensure the namespace is created for vLLM resources.

steps.title: Create an example vLLM Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for vLLM to serve a model.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This CR specifies the model and number of replicas to serve.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM Custom Resource
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Look for the status to be 'Running' for the example-vllm resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the vLLM installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data related to the vLLM Custom Resource.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command removes the Helm release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Custom Resources. This process involves draining the nodes if necessary, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Backup the current vLLM configuration.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM Custom Resource is running the new version.
commands: kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Custom Resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the vLLM controller with 'kubectl logs -l app=vllm -n vllm'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the model server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The model server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the status of the vLLM resource with 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the vLLM Custom Resource definition.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs with 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm' showing the example-vllm resource with a status of 'Running'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the required tools installed.
